ding_momo.description: Keep “Momo” for now rather than forcing “Nanny” or “Nurse.” Final treatment should stay aligned with the broader system for household titles and forms of address.

lady_wang.description: Use consistently. Do not alternate casually with Madam Wang unless a deliberate system change is made.

old_lady_qin.description: Senior matriarch figure in the Qin family.

qin_liuxi.description: Main character. Do not drift to "Qi Liuxi" or other variants.

chi_yuan_daoist.description: 赤=chi (pinyin for color red), rendered as pinyin here for name-like flow. Prefer this over "Red Yuan Daoist" (too descriptive) or "Daoist Red Yuan" (over-forced). Keep stable.

lady_gu.description: Third branch wife of the Qin family. Carrying twins, labor induced by the shock of the raid.

aunt_li.description: Senior household servant / lead maid attached to major women such as Lady Wang, Lady Gu, or Old Lady Qin. Not a blood-relative aunt — in this book the form refers to a senior household servant role.

doctor_zhang.description: Senior physician from Zhang Family Medical Hall in Annan Prefecture. Canonical rendering is 'Doctor Zhang' — not 'Dr. Zhang', not 'Zhang Dafu'. Keep stable.

zhang_zhuoliang.description: Doctor Zhang's grandson. Young physician, protective of family reputation.

doctor_gao.description: Supplementary physician at Li Family Village epidemic. Skeptical of Qin Liuxi.

doctor_yang.description: Supplementary physician at Li Family Village epidemic. Sarcastic toward Qin Liuxi.

ancestral_temple_rites.notes: Prefer a stable ritual-language rendering across the book.

auspicious_creature___auspicious_symbol_.name_en: auspicious creature / auspicious symbol / blessed symbol
auspicious_creature___auspicious_symbol_.notes: In founding-myth, dynastic, or ritual contexts, avoid modern "mascot" or "lucky mascot." Prefer historical-register wording that preserves the ritual/symbolic weight.

bleeding_from_the_seven_orifices.notes: Keep vivid but controlled. Do not embellish.

chief_minister_of_the_court_of_imperial_.name_en: Chief Minister of the Court of Imperial Entertainments
chief_minister_of_the_court_of_imperial_.notes: Provisional. Official title system needs later review for consistency and readability.

clashing_baleful_influence.notes: Provisional. Needs stable handling across fate / Daoist / metaphysical language.

clear_the_family_name.notes: In context, may need slight variation, but preserve the sense of formal reversal of injustice.

fate_pattern.notes: Prefer controlled metaphysical wording; do not make it sound like modern self-help language.

from_an_official_household___from_a_fami.name_en: from an official household / from a family of officials
from_an_official_household___from_a_fami.notes: Means the family held civil-service/official positions. Do not elevate to "noble lineage" or "aristocratic." The Qin family are officials, not hereditary nobles. Distinguish from noble/aristocratic status.

gave_a_formal_feminine_salute___made_a_f.name_en: gave a formal feminine salute / made a formal salute
gave_a_formal_feminine_salute___made_a_f.notes: Historical feminine ritual gesture (hands clasped at the side), not a generic Western bow. Do not flatten to "bowed." In running prose, wording may vary but must preserve the ritual-gesture sense.

great_feng.notes: Fictional dynasty name. 灃 = fēng, not hóng. Never render as "Great Hong" or "Great Hong Dynasty." Use consistently once stabilized.

legal_mother.notes: Rejected alternatives: “principal mother” (too legalistic/unnatural), plain “mother” (loses hierarchy). “legal mother” preserves the legal-wife / principal-wife distinction from concubine-born structure without sounding legalistic. Do not use “principal mother”.

main_branch.notes: Keep stable unless a better clan-system rendering is adopted consistently.

momo.notes: For now, prefer retaining “Momo” in role names such as “Ding Momo” rather than forcing “Nanny” or “Nurse.” Final treatment should stay aligned with the broader system for household titles and forms of address.

noble_consort.notes: Prefer stable court-rank terminology once adopted.

noble_refinement.notes: Provisional. Preserve status and moral-social texture; avoid flat modern phrasing.

registered_under_[someones]_name.notes: Keep the formal household / lineage sense. Do not flatten into casual family phrasing.

the_debt_of_giving_birth_and_raising_[so.name_en: the debt of giving birth and raising [someone]
the_debt_of_giving_birth_and_raising_[so.notes: Context-sensitive. Preserve the layered sense of obligation and upbringing.

the_debt_of_raising_[someone].notes: Related to, but not identical with, 生养之恩.

the_three_sacrificial_animals.notes: Ritual term. Do not paraphrase loosely if it becomes recurring.

whisper_in_the_emperors_ear.notes: Translate by function in running prose, not literally.

xi’er.name_zh: 丫头 / 西丫头 / 你这丫头
xi’er.notes: Canonical rendering is "Xi’er" — preserves the elder-to-younger familiar register while reading naturally in English dialogue. The character's given name serves as the consistent address form. Rejected alternatives: "girl" (too flat, fails register), "little girl" (infantilizing), "Little Xi" (over-Westernized). Do not use any of these rejected forms.

young_lady.notes: Keep as “Young Lady” for now. Do not map this mechanically onto British aristocratic forms such as “Lady + given name.” This is a household-status term, not a British courtesy title. In running prose, wording may vary slightly if rank order or family structure needs to be made clearer.

qingping_temple.notes: Canonical rendering is 'Qingping Temple' — not 'Qingping Abbey', 'Monastery', or 'Shrine'. Qin Liuxi's temple. Keep stable across all chapters.

doctor_title.notes: Canonical rendering is 'Doctor [Surname]' for physician characters (Doctor Zhang, Doctor Gao, Doctor Yang). Not 'Dr.' abbreviation and not 'Dafu' transliteration. Working choice pending cross-chapter validation.

legal_mother.rationale: preserves the legal-wife distinction from concubine-born structure without sounding legalistic

xi’er.rationale: preserves the elder-to-younger familiar register while reading naturally in English dialogue; the character's given name serves as a consistent address form
